claim.content: The collected real-world corpus is defined as the set of Python-based tools obtained by crawling three named public tool platforms (mcp.so, mcpservers, and mcpmarket), resolving and retaining publicly accessible GitHub repositories with identifiable entry points, and filtering for accessibility and English descriptions to yield $N=10{,}573$ tools. The weak assumption is that this corpus constitutes a representative sample of the deployed LLM-agent tool ecosystem for the purposes of evaluating detection performance and embedding realism—i.e., that the observed category distributions and code-size statistics (for example, 88% of tools under 100 lines of code) and the functional diversity in the collected set reflect typical production tooling rather than being heavily biased by platform-specific selection, crawler heuristics, or filtering decisions.
